name: Fluke: PNW Orca Atlas
subtitle: Sightings, whales, and atlas
description:
Explore sourced killer whale sightings, individual whale profiles, and historical context across the Pacific Northwest.

LIVE SIGHTINGS
Browse a fresh, attributed community and provider feed. Explicit sighting submission sends its contact email, selected location and content, and attached photo to Fluke for moderation. Queued drafts stay on device until Submit is tapped. Optional account use separately sends Name, account email, and User ID. Sign in with Apple sends an identity token and one-use authorization code for authentication.

LIVE CAMERA FRAMING
Open the live camera to frame an orca dorsal fin with a private on-device preview. Camera frames stay on device; nothing is captured or uploaded from the preview, and the camera opens only after an explicit tap. Live matching of orca individuals is not active in this release: Fluke ships its dorsal-fin matching engine dormant until a rights-cleared reference catalog is certified, and identification claims will appear only when that is real.

WHALES AND ATLAS
Search the catalog, read evidence-backed profiles, and explore historical ranges and movement context. Confidence and source attribution stay visible.

Fluke has no advertising, analytics, or tracking. It is an independent reference project, not an emergency alert service. Never approach or pursue wildlife; follow current official viewing regulations.
keywords: orca,killer whales,dorsal fin,sightings,Salish Sea,catalog,atlas,field guide,whale watching
promotionalText: Follow live PNW orca sightings, frame dorsal fins with a private on-device camera preview, and browse evidence-backed whale profiles without analytics or tracking.
whatsNew: Version 1.1 adds a live community sightings feed with provider freshness, sighting submission with offline queueing and moderation, optional Sign in with Apple with a personal logbook, and a live on-device camera framing preview for dorsal fins. The dorsal-fin matching engine ships dormant: live matching stays off until a rights-cleared reference catalog is certified. Camera frames stay on device; explicit sighting submissions send their contact email, chosen location and content, and attached photo.
reviewNotes:
This build intentionally ships Fluke's on-device dorsal-fin matching engine dormant: no reference catalog is bundled, live matching is off, and the app makes no identification claims. The Identify tab provides a live camera framing preview only. Camera frames stay on device and are neither captured nor uploaded from the preview; the camera opens only after an explicit tap and never requests permission before that tap. A future release enables matching only after a rights-cleared catalog is certified by the release verifier (ready:true).

Only explicit sighting submission sends its contact email, user-chosen location and content, and attached photo for moderation. Fluke has no analytics or tracking. Optional account use separately sends Name, account email, and User ID. Sign in with Apple sends its identity token and one-use authorization code for authentication.

Suggested review path:
1. Browse Sightings and Whales.
2. Open Identify: a "Live matching not active" notice is shown; tap "Open live camera" for the on-device framing preview.
3. Draft a sighting and verify no network submission occurs until Submit is tapped.
4. Submit only test content suitable for moderation.

Live API: https://fluke-api.onrender.com
Support: https://fluke-pnw.vercel.app/support
Privacy: https://fluke-pnw.vercel.app/privacy
